3.why: The rotation works on an n by n grid.

4.happening: left marks the outer layer at index {left}.
4.why: We rotate one concentric ring at a time.
4.invariant: left is the layer's low boundary.

5.happening: right marks the far boundary at index {right}.
5.why: left and right bound the current ring.
5.invariant: right is the layer's high boundary.

6.why: A ring with width is still worth rotating.
6.invariant: When left meets right, all rings are done.

7.happening: Rotate the cell at offset {offset} along the top edge.
7.why: Each offset moves one group of four cells.
7.invariant: One offset handles four cells at once.

8.happening: The top row of this ring is {top}.
8.why: Corners are computed from top/bottom and offset.

9.happening: The bottom row of this ring is {bottom}.
9.why: It anchors the lower two cells of the group.

10.happening: Save the top-left value before it is overwritten.
10.why: It would otherwise be lost in the cycle.

11.happening: Bottom-left moves up into the top-left.
11.why: Each cell shifts one quarter-turn clockwise.

14.happening: The saved value moves into the top-right.
14.invariant: All four cells are now rotated.

15.happening: Shrink the ring inward from the left.
15.invariant: Move to the next inner ring.

16.happening: Shrink the ring inward from the right.
16.invariant: Move to the next inner ring.

init.why: Prepare to rotate layer by layer.

loop.happening: Advance to the next ring or offset.

update.why: This performs the rotation in place.
update.invariant: Four cells rotate as a group.

move.happening: Shrink to the next inner ring.
